messages.content: You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReverseRunes (returns the string with its runes reversed, keeping multi-byte UTF-8 characters intact).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for TruncateRunes (returns at most limit runes from the start of s without splitting a multi-byte character; a non-positive limit yields the empty string).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SwapCase (swaps the case of every cased letter, leaving uncased runes unchanged).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsLetterPalindrome (reports whether the letters of s form a palindrome, ignoring case and any non-letter runes).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RuneFrequency (returns a map counting how many times each rune occurs in s).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CapitalizeWords (upper-cases the first rune of each whitespace-delimited word and lower-cases the rest, preserving the original whitespace).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ChunkByRunes (splits s into consecutive chunks of at most size runes each; returns nil when size is non-positive or s is empty).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CountWords (returns the number of whitespace-delimited words in s, treating any Unicode whitespace as a separator).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Map[T, U any](s []T, f func(T) U) []U (returns a new slice applying f to each element in order; a nil input yields a nil result).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Filter[T any](s []T, keep func(T) bool) []T (returns a new slice of the elements for which keep returns true, preserving order; nil result when nothing is kept).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Reduce[T, A any](s []T, init A, f func(A, T) A) A (left-to-right fold of s into a single accumulator starting from init; returns init unchanged for an empty or nil slice).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Chunk[T any](s []T, size int) [][]T (splits s into consecutive sub-slices of at most size elements, the last possibly shorter; returns nil when size <= 0).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Unique[T comparable](s []T) []T (returns a new slice with duplicates removed, keeping the first occurrence of each value and preserving first-seen order).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for GroupBy[K comparable, T any](s []T, key func(T) K) map[K][]T (groups elements into a map by key(element), preserving order within each group; the returned map is always non-nil).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Partition[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) (matched, rest []T) (splits s into the elements matching pred and the remainder, each preserving original order; an empty side is nil).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Flatten[T any](s [][]T) []T (concatenates the inner slices in order into one new slice; empty and nil inner slices contribute nothing, and the result is nil when no elements remain).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for WordFrequency (counts how many times each whitespace-separated word occurs in a string, returning a map[string]int).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SetIntersection (returns the sorted, distinct ints present in both input slices).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SymmetricDifference (returns the sorted, distinct ints present in exactly one of two input slices).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for InvertMap (swaps keys and values of a map[string]int, returning the inverted map[int]string and ok=false with a nil map if two keys share a value).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MergeMaps (variadic; merges any number of map[string]int into a new map where the later map wins on key conflicts and inputs are not mutated).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsSubset (reports whether every key of one set map[string]struct{} is present in another).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for GroupByLength (groups words by their rune length into a map[int][]string, preserving input order within each group).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for FirstNonRepeating (returns the first rune that appears exactly once in a string, with ok=false if every rune repeats or the string is empty).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for AddOverflow(a, b int64) (int64, bool) (returns a+b and reports whether the signed int64 addition overflowed).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MulOverflow(a, b int32) (int32, bool) (returns a*b truncated to int32 and reports whether the signed multiplication overflowed the int32 range).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Abs(x int32) (int32, bool) (returns the absolute value of x with ok=true, except for math.MinInt32 whose magnitude is not representable as an int32, which returns (x, false)).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SafeDiv(a, b int64) (int64, error) (returns the truncated quotient a/b, returning the sentinel ErrDivideByZero when b==0 and ErrOverflow for math.MinInt64 / -1).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for GCD(a, b int) int (Euclidean greatest common divisor taken by magnitude so signs are ignored, with GCD(0, 0) defined as 0).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsPowerOfTwo(n uint64) bool (reports whether n is a power of two such as 1, 2, 4, 8; zero is not a power of two).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for AddUint8(a, b uint8) (uint8, bool) (returns the 8-bit wraparound sum a+b and reports whether the addition carried out of the 8-bit range).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Pow(base, exp int64) (int64, bool) (returns base**exp for non-negative exp with ok=true, returning (0, false) on a negative exponent or when the result overflows int64).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for HumanizeDuration (formats a time.Duration as a compact human-readable string like "1d2h3m4s", omitting zero units, rendering sub-second magnitudes as "0s", and prefixing negatives with "-").
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsLeapYear (reports whether an int year is a leap year in the proleptic Gregorian calendar: divisible by 4, except centuries unless divisible by 400).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for DaysInMonth (returns the number of days in a given time.Month of a given year, accounting for leap years).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for BusinessDaysBetween (counts weekday dates, Monday-Friday, in the half-open interval [start, end) comparing by calendar date; returns 0 when end is not after start).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for OverlapDuration (returns the time.Duration overlap of two half-open intervals [aStart,aEnd) and [bStart,bEnd), or 0 when they do not overlap or only touch at an endpoint).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for NextWeekday (returns midnight in t's location of the first calendar day strictly after t's date whose weekday is wd; if t already falls on wd, the result is seven days later).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseClock (parses a 24-hour "HH:MM:SS" string into hour, minute, and second ints, returning an error on bad format or out-of-range values).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Age (returns the whole number of years between birth and asOf like a person's age, accounting for whether the birthday has occurred yet that year, and returning 0 when asOf precedes birth).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for WrapErr (wraps an error with a context message using fmt.Errorf %w, returning nil when the input error is nil; the result must support errors.Unwrap and errors.Is).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RootCause (repeatedly unwraps an error via errors.Unwrap and returns the deepest non-nil error, or nil for a nil input; a %v-formatted error has no Unwrap and is its own root).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for NewValidationError (returns a *ValidationError that records a Field and wraps a cause via Unwrap, so callers can use errors.As to read the Field and errors.Is to match the cause; Error() reports the field and cause).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ValidateUser (validates name and age, joining all violations with errors.Join so each sentinel — ErrEmptyName, ErrNegativeAge, ErrTooOld — is matchable with errors.Is; returns nil when the input is valid, and age 150 is allowed).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParsePort (parses a TCP port string; wraps strconv.ErrSyntax for non-numeric input and ErrPortRange for values outside 1..65535, preserving context with %w; returns 0 on any error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CodeOf (walks the error chain with errors.As and returns the Code of the first *CodedError found, or 0 when none is present; the outermost CodedError's code wins).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ChainLen (counts the errors in an error's single-Unwrap chain, including the error itself; returns 0 for nil and does not descend into %v-formatted errors).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsTimeout (reports whether any error in the chain implements interface{ Timeout() bool } and returns true from it, using errors.As; an error that implements the interface but returns false is not a timeout).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsSorted (reports whether an []int is sorted in non-decreasing order; empty and single-element slices count as sorted).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MergeSorted (merges two ascending-sorted []int into one new ascending-sorted slice; inputs are not modified).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for KthSmallest (returns the k-th smallest element of an []int using 1-based ranking; returns an error if k is outside [1, len]).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SortByLength (returns a new []string ordered by length shortest-first, stable on equal lengths; input is not modified).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for BinarySearch (returns the index of target in an ascending-sorted []int, or -1 if target is absent).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Dedup (returns a new ascending-sorted []int of the distinct values; input is not modified; result is always non-nil).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Median (returns the median of a []float64 — the middle value for odd counts, the average of the two middle values for even counts; panics on empty input; input is not modified).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for TopK (returns the k largest elements of an []int in descending order; an empty slice if k<=0; all elements if k>=len; input is not modified).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MarshalUser (encodes a User{Name, Email, Age} struct to JSON; Email and Age use `,omitempty`, and the default encoder HTML-escapes <, >, & while preserving unicode).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for UnmarshalPoint (decodes JSON bytes into a Point{X, Y int} with json.Unmarshal; missing fields keep their zero value, unknown fields are ignored, key matching is case-insensitive, JSON null leaves the struct untouched, and malformed/empty/wrong-type input returns an error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for IsValidJSON (reports whether a byte slice is exactly one complete, well-formed JSON value via json.Valid; surrounding whitespace is allowed, but empty input, trailing garbage, two concatenated values, and syntax errors are invalid).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for PrettyJSON (renders any value as a two-space-indented JSON string via json.MarshalIndent; map keys are sorted, nesting deepens the indent, empty composites stay on one line, and unencodable values such as channels or funcs return an error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CompactJSON (strips insignificant whitespace from a JSON document via json.Compact while preserving whitespace inside string literals; already-compact input is unchanged and invalid/empty input returns an error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for DecodeRecords (streams whitespace-separated or directly concatenated JSON objects from an io.Reader into a []Record{ID int, Name string} using a json.Decoder loop until io.EOF; an empty or whitespace-only stream yields no records and a malformed/wrong-type object returns an error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SumJSONNumbers (decodes a JSON array of integers and returns their int64 sum using a json.Decoder with UseNumber/json.Number so values beyond float64's 53-bit mantissa stay exact; empty array and JSON null sum to zero, while non-integer, non-numeric, or malformed input returns an error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RGB.MarshalJSON and RGB.UnmarshalJSON (an RGB{R,G,B uint8} color type whose custom codec serializes to and parses from a lowercase "#rrggbb" hex JSON string; cover zero-padding, upper/lower hex, round-tripping inside a larger struct, and errors for wrong length, missing '#', non-hex digits, and non-string JSON).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReverseBytes (returns a new slice with the bytes of the input in reverse order, without modifying the input).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CountNonOverlapping (returns the number of non-overlapping occurrences of sep in s; returns 0 when sep is empty).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ChunkBytes (splits data into consecutive chunks of at most size bytes, where the last chunk may be shorter; returns nil when size <= 0 or data is empty).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for TrimTrailingZeros (returns a sub-slice of b with all trailing 0x00 bytes removed, preserving leading and interior zero bytes).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for EscapeWhitespace (returns a copy of data in which each newline, carriage return and tab byte is replaced by its two-character backslash escape: \n, \r, \t).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RunLengthEncode (encodes data as (count, value) byte pairs with each count in the range 1..255, splitting any run longer than 255 across multiple pairs).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for LongestCommonPrefix (returns a new slice holding the longest byte sequence that is a prefix of both a and b).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SplitKeyValue (splits line on the first occurrence of sep, returning the bytes before it as key, the bytes after it as value, and true; returns (line, nil, false) when sep is absent).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for SafeCounter (a goroutine-safe counter built on sync.Mutex, with Inc, Add(delta), and Value; the zero value is usable).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Merge (fan-in: merges several <-chan int inputs into one output channel using sync.WaitGroup, closing the output once every input is drained).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Lazy (a NewLazy(init)/Get lazy initializer built on sync.Once that computes its value exactly once, even under concurrent callers, and caches it).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParallelMap (applies f to each element of a slice concurrently using up to n worker goroutines fed by a jobs channel, returning results in input order; n<1 is clamped to 1).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RWCache (a goroutine-safe string-to-int cache guarded by sync.RWMutex, with Set, Get returning (value, ok), Delete returning whether the key existed, and Len).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Future (an asynchronous result from NewFuture(f) that runs f in its own goroutine and signals completion by closing a done channel; Await blocks and is repeatable/concurrent-safe, Done reports completion without blocking).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RunAll (runs each func() error in its own goroutine, waits with sync.WaitGroup, and returns the resulting errors in input order; a nil function entry yields a nil error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MinHeap (a slice-backed binary min-heap with Push, Pop, Peek, and Len; Pop and Peek return (int, bool) where ok is false when the heap is empty).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for PriorityQueue (a max-priority queue of string values built on container/heap; NewPriorityQueue, Push(value string, priority int), Pop() (string, bool), and Len; the highest-priority value pops first).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReverseList (reverse a singly linked list of *ListNode{Val int; Next *ListNode} in place and return the new head; nil for an empty list).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MiddleNode (return the middle node of a singly linked list via slow/fast pointers; for an even count return the second of the two middle nodes; nil for an empty list).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MergeTwoLists (merge two ascending-sorted singly linked lists into one ascending-sorted list and return its head; either input may be nil).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for RingBuffer (a fixed-capacity FIFO ring buffer; NewRingBuffer(capacity), Enqueue(int) bool returns false when full, Dequeue() (int, bool) returns false when empty, plus Len and Cap).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CircularBuffer (a most-recent-N ring buffer that overwrites the oldest element when full; NewCircularBuffer(size), Add(int) (evicted int, didEvict bool), Slice() returning elements oldest-to-newest, and Len).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for MinStack (a LIFO int stack that reports its current minimum in O(1); NewMinStack, Push, and Pop/Top/Min each returning (int, bool) with ok=false when empty, plus Len).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseBool (parses a flexible boolean from true/false/yes/no/1/0/on/off, case-insensitively and trimming surrounding whitespace; returns an error for anything else).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ValidateEmail (reports whether a string is a syntactically valid email: exactly one '@', a non-empty local part, and a domain containing a dot that is neither the first nor last character).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseIPv4 (parses a dotted-quad IPv4 string into a [4]byte, erroring on the wrong octet count, empty or non-numeric octets, or any octet greater than 255).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseKeyValues (parses semicolon-separated key=value pairs into a map[string]string, trimming spaces and skipping empty segments; errors on a segment with no '=' or an empty key).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for LuhnValid (reports whether a digit string passes the Luhn checksum, returning false for empty input or any non-digit character).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseSemVer (parses a strict MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH version into three ints, rejecting wrong field counts, empty or non-numeric components, and leading zeros).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ValidateHexColor (reports whether a string is a CSS hex color: a '#' followed by exactly 3 or 6 hexadecimal digits).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ParseCSVLine (splits a single CSV line into fields, honoring double-quoted fields where commas are literal and ""-escaped quotes are unescaped; errors on an unterminated quote).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReadLines (reads all newline-separated lines from an io.Reader using bufio.Scanner, strips line terminators, produces no trailing empty line, and returns any non-EOF read error).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CountWords (counts whitespace-separated words read from an io.Reader using a bufio.Scanner with bufio.ScanWords; ignores leading/trailing whitespace and collapses runs of separators).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReadFullBlock (reads exactly n bytes from an io.Reader via io.ReadFull; returns io.EOF if no bytes are read, io.ErrUnexpectedEOF on a short read, and an empty slice with nil error when n==0).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for WriteLines (writes each string followed by '\n' to an io.Writer through a bufio.Writer, flushes before returning, and reports the total number of bytes written including the newline terminators).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for ReadUntil (reads from an io.Reader up to and including the first occurrence of a delim byte using bufio.Reader.ReadString; when delim is absent it returns the data read so far together with io.EOF).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for LimitedRead (reads at most limit bytes from an io.Reader by wrapping it with io.LimitReader and draining via io.ReadAll; stops at EOF or the limit, whichever comes first).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Concat (reads the contents of variadic io.Readers in order using io.MultiReader and returns them concatenated as one string; returns "" when given no readers).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for CountingWriter (an io.Writer wrapper, built with NewCountingWriter, that forwards each write to an underlying writer and accumulates the total number of bytes written in its Total field).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Write a thorough table-driven Go test (package sandbox) for Transition (a turnstile finite state machine: `func Transition(s State, ev Event) (State, error)` where State is the enum Locked/Unlocked and Event is the enum Coin/Push; a Coin always unlocks, a Push from Unlocked locks and lets one person through, and a Push from Locked is rejected with an error leaving the state Locked).
You are GuildLM go-test, a Go testing specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
